api: Tremendous API v2 (OpenAPI 3.0.2 spec embedded per-endpoint at https://developers.tremendous.com/reference/<slug>.md, fetched 2026-07-03; servers: https://testflight.tremendous.com/api/v2 sandbox, https://api.tremendous.com/api/v2 production)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion. Covered: the 4 legacy-parity read streams (campaigns, orders, rewards, funding_sources, unchanged) plus 3 new Pass B streams (products, invoices, members) and 11 new write actions (create_order, approve_order, reject_order, cancel_reward, resend_reward, generate_reward_link, create_invoice, delete_invoice, create_member, create_webhook, delete_webhook) covering every dialect-expressible mutation Tremendous documents outside organization/connected-org/fraud-rule administration. Excluded: Tremendous Connect embedded-partner-platform administration (connected organizations/members, a distinct product requiring separate enablement), fraud-review/fraud-rule security administration, organization self-administration (create sub-org, create API key), async report-generation jobs (no declarative one-shot representation), binary invoice CSV/PDF exports, topups (an alternate account-funding mechanism distinct from the invoices flow this pass covers), campaign creation/update (config-heavy admin action deferred as a follow-on), balance_transactions (no stable id field in Tremendous's own schema to key a stream on), and single-item detail/duplicate-of-list endpoints already covered by their corresponding list stream.

excluded.reason: balance_transactions has no stable id field in Tremendous's own published schema (created_at/amount/action/description/order only) — there is no real API property to declare as x-primary-key without inventing a synthetic composite key the API itself does not expose; the balance_transactions.order sub-object already duplicates data the orders stream covers

campaign creation was not modeled by legacy and is a config-heavy (branding/message/product-selection) admin action better reviewed as its own follow-on rather than folded into this pass; the existing campaigns stream already covers read access to the resulting objects
campaign update (branding/message/product-selection admin config) was not modeled by legacy; a distinct follow-on decision from this pass's read/order/reward/invoice/member/webhook write scope

custom-field definition list (reporting/analytics tagging metadata for rewards); config metadata, not primary reward/order object data

custom-field definitions must first be added by an admin via the Tremendous Dashboard per Tremendous's own docs; the API-side create is a narrow admin-config action layered on top of order/reward data this pass already covers
static currency exchange-rate reference lookup; not an account object stream

fraud-review/fraud-rule administration is an organization security/risk function distinct from ordinary reward data; blocking/releasing a fraud-held reward or reconfiguring fraud rules is a security-sensitive admin action out of this pass's create/read scope for reward and order data

single invoice detail lookup by id; the invoices stream's list endpoint already returns this record's full field set, and per Tremendous's own docs this endpoint's only additional value (retrieving already-deleted invoices) is a narrow edge case not worth a dedicated stream
CSV export of an invoice's associated rewards/orders; a binary/text-file download payload with no declarative dialect representation
PDF rendering of an invoice; binary payload with no declarative dialect representation
single member detail lookup by id; the members stream's list endpoint already returns this record's full field set
single order detail lookup by id; the orders stream's list endpoint already returns this record's full field set
per Tremendous's own docs, this always returns exactly the single organization the calling API key belongs to; not a syncable multi-record object stream
creates a new sub-organization within the Tremendous account; organization-administration action, not reward/order/campaign object data
creates a new API key for the organization; a credential-issuance action, never a reverse-ETL write, and unrelated to the API key this connector itself authenticates with
single product detail lookup by id; the products stream's list endpoint already returns this record's full field set

kicks off an asynchronous report-generation job (polled via GET or a REPORTS webhook event); an async job-queue interaction pattern with no declarative one-shot stream/write representation in this dialect
polls the status/download-URL of an async report job; only meaningful paired with the create_report job-kickoff this pass does not model
single reward detail lookup by id; the rewards stream's list endpoint already returns this record's full field set
static list of role IDs available for the create_member/update-member role field; reference/lookup data for member administration, not primary reward/order/campaign object data
topup list; topups are out of scope for the reason above

an alternate account-balance-funding mechanism (a recurring auto-reload configuration) distinct from the one-time invoice flow this pass already covers via create_invoice/delete_invoice; a follow-on decision, not core reward/order data
single topup detail; topups are out of scope for the reason above

returns the organization's single webhook (Tremendous supports exactly one per organization); the create_webhook/delete_webhook writes already round-trip this same object, and there is no meaningful multi-record list to stream
per Tremendous's own docs an organization can only ever have exactly ONE webhook; the plural list-webhooks endpoint already returns that same single webhook object
static list of event-type names that can be sent to a webhook; reference/lookup data, not account object data
triggers a synthetic test webhook delivery for the caller's own integration-testing purposes; a developer-testing utility action, not a reverse-ETL object mutation
